Perfect! Now I have comprehensive information. Let me compile the findings into a structured report:

## COMPREHENSIVE CODEBASE EXPLORATION REPORT

### 1. DIRECTORY STRUCTURE

**Top-level directories:**
- `/cmd/` â€” Executable entry points
  - `cmd/server/` â€” HTTP API server
  - `cmd/agent/` â€” Worker for agent CLI
- `/internal/` â€” Core business logic (packages)
  - `internal/api/` â€” HTTP handlers & API routes
  - `internal/db/` â€” Database schema & migrations
  - `internal/ingestion/` â€” Polling sources for release discovery
  - `internal/models/` â€” Domain types
  - `internal/queue/` â€” River job queue job definitions
  - `internal/routing/` â€” Notification channels (Slack, Discord, webhook)
  - `internal/agent/` â€” ADK-Go agent orchestration & tools
    - `internal/agent/openai/` â€” OpenAI LLM provider
- `/web/` â€” Next.js frontend
  - `web/app/` â€” Next.js App Router pages
  - `web/components/` â€” React components
  - `web/lib/` â€” Client utilities & API wrappers
- `/docs/` â€” Documentation
  - `docs/plans/` â€” Planning & lesson records
  - `docs/designs/` â€” Architecture docs
- `/scripts/` â€” Build/test scripts
- `/deployments/` â€” Deployment configs

---

### 2. API ROUTES (Complete)

**Base URL:** `/api/v1`

#### Projects
- `GET /projects` â€” List all projects (paginated)
- `POST /projects` â€” Create new project
- `GET /projects/{id}` â€” Get project by ID
- `PUT /projects/{id}` â€” Update project
- `DELETE /projects/{id}` â€” Delete project

#### Sources (nested under projects)
- `GET /projects/{projectId}/sources` â€” List sources for project
- `POST /projects/{projectId}/sources` â€” Create source for project
- `GET /sources/{id}` â€” Get source by ID
- `PUT /sources/{id}` â€” Update source
- `DELETE /sources/{id}` â€” Delete source
- `POST /sources/{id}/poll` â€” Manually trigger source polling

#### Releases (read-only, nested)
- `GET /releases` â€” List all releases across all projects
- `GET /sources/{id}/releases` â€” List releases from a source
- `GET /projects/{projectId}/releases` â€” List releases for a project
- `GET /releases/{id}` â€” Get release by ID

#### Semantic Releases (nested + top-level)
- `GET /semantic-releases` â€” List all semantic releases
- `GET /projects/{projectId}/semantic-releases` â€” List semantic releases for project
- `GET /semantic-releases/{id}` â€” Get semantic release by ID
- `GET /semantic-releases/{id}/sources` â€” List source releases that compose a semantic release
- `DELETE /semantic-releases/{id}` â€” Delete semantic release

#### Context Sources (nested under projects)
- `GET /projects/{projectId}/context-sources` â€” List context sources for project
- `POST /projects/{projectId}/context-sources` â€” Create context source
- `GET /context-sources/{id}` â€” Get context source by ID
- `PUT /context-sources/{id}` â€” Update context source
- `DELETE /context-sources/{id}` â€” Delete context source

#### Subscriptions (CRUD)
- `GET /subscriptions` â€” List all subscriptions
- `POST /subscriptions` â€” Create subscription
- `GET /subscriptions/{id}` â€” Get subscription by ID
- `PUT /subscriptions/{id}` â€” Update subscription
- `DELETE /subscriptions/{id}` â€” Delete subscription

#### Notification Channels (CRUD + test)
- `GET /channels` â€” List all notification channels
- `POST /channels` â€” Create channel
- `GET /channels/{id}` â€” Get channel by ID
- `PUT /channels/{id}` â€” Update channel
- `DELETE /channels/{id}` â€” Delete channel
- `POST /channels/{id}/test` â€” Send test notification to verify channel

#### Agent
- `POST /projects/{projectId}/agent/run` â€” Trigger new agent run for project
- `GET /projects/{projectId}/agent/runs` â€” List agent runs for project
- `GET /agent-runs/{id}` â€” Get agent run by ID

#### Metadata
- `GET /providers` â€” List supported ingestion providers

#### Server-Sent Events (SSE)
- `GET /events` â€” Open SSE stream for real-time release/semantic release events

#### Health & Stats
- `GET /health` â€” Health check (database ping)
- `GET /stats` â€” Aggregate dashboard statistics
- `GET /stats/trend` â€” Time-bucketed release counts (configurable granularity)

**Middleware chain:**
- Public routes: `RequestID` â†’ `Logger` â†’ `Recovery`
- Protected routes: + `RateLimit(10 rps, 20 burst)` + `Auth` (unless `NO_AUTH=true`)

---

### 3. DATA MODELS (Domain Types)

**Internal package:** `internal/models/`

| Type | Key Fields | Purpose |
|------|-----------|---------|
| `Project` | `ID`, `Name`, `Description`, `AgentPrompt`, `AgentRules`, `CreatedAt`, `UpdatedAt` | Central entity: tracked software project |
| `Source` | `ID`, `ProjectID`, `Provider`, `Repository`, `PollIntervalSeconds`, `Enabled`, `Config`, `LastPolledAt`, `LastError` | Configured ingestion source (polling-based) |
| `Release` | `ID`, `SourceID`, `Version`, `RawData`, `ReleasedAt`, `CreatedAt` | Source-level release detected by polling |
| `SemanticRelease` | `ID`, `ProjectID`, `Version`, `Report`, `Status`, `Error`, `CreatedAt`, `CompletedAt` | AI-generated project-level report (status: pending/completed) |
| `SemanticReport` (nested) | `Subject`, `RiskLevel`, `RiskReason`, `StatusChecks`, `ChangelogSummary`, `DownloadCommands`, `DownloadLinks`, `Summary`, `Availability`, `Adoption`, `Urgency`, `Recommendation` | SRE-focused analysis of semantic release |
| `NotificationChannel` | `ID`, `Name`, `Type`, `Config`, `CreatedAt`, `UpdatedAt` | Standalone notification endpoint (Slack, Discord, webhook) |
| `Subscription` | `ID`, `ChannelID`, `Type`, `SourceID`, `ProjectID`, `VersionFilter`, `CreatedAt` | Maps channel to source or project with optional version filter |
| `ContextSource` | `ID`, `ProjectID`, `Type`, `Name`, `Config`, `CreatedAt`, `UpdatedAt` | Read-only reference for agent research (runbooks, docs, dashboards) |
| `AgentRun` | `ID`, `ProjectID`, `SemanticReleaseID`, `Trigger`, `Version`, `Status`, `PromptUsed`, `Error`, `StartedAt`, `CompletedAt`, `CreatedAt` | Agent execution record scoped to project |
| `AgentRules` (JSON struct) | `OnMajorRelease`, `OnMinorRelease`, `OnSecurityPatch`, `VersionPattern`, `WaitForAllSources` | Decision rules for triggering agent on project |

---

### 4. DATABASE SCHEMA

**File:** `/internal/db/migrations.go`

Tables created by `RunMigrations()` (idempotent):

| Table | Key Columns | Purpose |
|-------|------------|---------|
| `projects` | `id` (PK UUID), `name` (UNIQUE), `agent_prompt`, `agent_rules` (JSONB) | Tracked projects |
| `sources` | `id` (PK), `project_id` (FK), `provider`, `repository`, `poll_interval_seconds`, `enabled`, `config` (JSONB), `last_polled_at`, `last_error` | Ingestion sources per project; UNIQUE on `(provider, repository)` |
| `context_sources` | `id` (PK), `project_id` (FK), `type`, `name`, `config` (JSONB) | Agent research references |
| `releases` | `id` (PK), `source_id` (FK), `version`, `raw_data` (JSONB), `released_at` | Source releases; UNIQUE on `(source_id, version)` |
| `semantic_releases` | `id` (PK), `project_id` (FK), `version`, `report` (JSONB), `status` (pending/completed), `error`, `completed_at` | AI-generated reports; UNIQUE on `(project_id, version)` |
| `semantic_release_sources` | `semantic_release_id` (FK), `release_id` (FK) | Join table linking source releases â†’ semantic release |
| `notification_channels` | `id` (PK), `name`, `type`, `config` (JSONB) | Slack/Discord/webhook endpoints |
| `subscriptions` | `id` (PK), `channel_id` (FK), `type` (source/project), `source_id` (FK), `project_id` (FK), `version_filter` | Channel â†” source/project mappings; CHECK ensures correct type relationship |
| `agent_runs` | `id` (PK), `project_id` (FK), `semantic_release_id` (FK), `trigger`, `version`, `status` (pending/completed), `prompt_used`, `error`, `started_at`, `completed_at` | Agent execution records |
| `api_keys` | `id` (PK), `name`, `key_hash` (UNIQUE), `key_prefix`, `created_at`, `last_used_at` | API authentication |

**River tables:** Created by `rivermigrate` (queue state, job history)

**Triggers & Functions:**
- `notify_release_created()` â†’ `NOTIFY release_events` on new release (feeds SSE)
- `notify_semantic_release()` â†’ `NOTIFY release_events` on semantic release completion

---

### 5. KEY INTERFACES

**API (in `internal/api/`):**

| Interface | Methods | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|---------|
| `ProjectsStore` | `ListProjects`, `CreateProject`, `GetProject`, `UpdateProject`, `DeleteProject` | Project persistence |
| `SourcesStore` | `ListSourcesForProject`, `CreateSource`, `GetSource`, `UpdateSource`, `DeleteSource`, `GetSourcesForPolling` | Source persistence |
| `ReleasesStore` | `ListAllReleases`, `ListReleasesBySource`, `ListReleasesByProject`, `GetRelease` | Release queries |
| `SemanticReleasesStore` | `ListSemanticReleases`, `ListSemanticReleasesForProject`, `GetSemanticRelease`, `DeleteSemanticRelease`, `ListSourcesForSemanticRelease` | Semantic release persistence |
| `SubscriptionsStore` | `ListSubscriptions`, `CreateSubscription`, `GetSubscription`, `UpdateSubscription`, `DeleteSubscription` | Subscription persistence |
| `ChannelsStore` | `ListChannels`, `CreateChannel`, `GetChannel`, `UpdateChannel`, `DeleteChannel` | Channel persistence |
| `ContextSourcesStore` | `ListContextSources`, `CreateContextSource`, `GetContextSource`, `UpdateContextSource`, `DeleteContextSource` | Context source persistence |
| `AgentStore` | `TriggerAgentRun`, `ListAgentRuns`, `GetAgentRun`, `CreateSemanticRelease`, `UpdateSemanticRelease`, `ListReleasesByProject`, `GetRelease`, `ListContextSources` | Agent operations & data access |
| `KeyStore` | `ValidateKey`, `TouchKeyUsage` | API key authentication |
| `HealthChecker` | `PingDB`, `GetStats`, `GetTrend` | Health & stats |

**Ingestion (in `internal/ingestion/`):**

| Interface | Methods | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|---------|
| `IIngestionSource` | `Name()`, `SourceID()`, `FetchNewReleases(ctx)` | Polling-based release provider |
| `ReleaseStore` (internal) | `IngestRelease` | Transactional outbox pattern (insert release + enqueue notify job in single TX) |

**Routing (in `internal/routing/`):**

| Interface | Methods | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|---------|
| `Sender` | `Send(ctx, channel, msg)` | Notification channel implementation |
| `NotifyStore` (internal) | Data access for notification worker | Query releases, subscriptions for notifications |

**Agent (in `internal/agent/`):**

| Interface | Methods | Purpose |
|-----------|---------|---------|
| `AgentDataStore` | `ListReleasesByProject`, `GetRelease`, `ListContextSources` | Data access for agent tools |
| `OrchestratorStore` (internal) | Full data access for agent | Project, agent runs, semantic releases, context sources |

---

### 6. AGENT TOOLS (ADK-Go)

**File:** `/internal/agent/tools.go`

Three project-scoped tools created by `NewTools(store, projectID)`:

1. **`get_releases`** â€” Fetch paginated releases for the project
   - Input: `GetReleasesInput` (page, perPage)
   - Output: `GetReleasesOutput` (releases array, total, page, perPage)
   - Use case: Agent researches recent releases

2. **`get_release_detail`** â€” Get detailed info for a specific release
   - Input: `GetReleaseDetailInput` (release_id)
   - Output: `GetReleaseDetailOutput` (full release with raw_data)
   - Use case: Inspect changelog, commit lists, metadata from upstream

3. **`list_context_sources`** â€” Fetch context sources for the project
   - Input: `ListContextSourcesInput` (page, perPage)
   - Output: `ListContextSourcesOutput` (sources array with id, type, name, config)
   - Use case: Access runbooks, docs, dashboards for richer analysis

---

### 7. QUEUE JOBS (River v0.31.0)

**File:** `/internal/queue/jobs.go`

| Job Type | Kind | Args | Trigger | Worker |
|----------|------|------|---------|--------|
| `NotifyJobArgs` | `"notify_release"` | `ReleaseID`, `SourceID` | Enqueued in `IngestRelease()` TX when new release detected | `NotifyWorker` in `internal/routing/worker.go` |
| `AgentJobArgs` | `"agent_run"` | `AgentRunID`, `ProjectID`, `Version` | Enqueued by NotifyWorker when agent rules match OR manually triggered via `/agent/run` POST | `AgentWorker` in `internal/agent/worker.go` |

**Data integrity:** Transactional outbox pattern â€” release insert + job enqueue happen in same SQL TX.

---

### 8. ENVIRONMENT VARIABLES

**Set in `cmd/server/main.go`:**

| Variable | Default | Description |
|----------|---------|-------------|
| `DATABASE_URL` | `postgres://localhost:5432/changelogue?sslmode=disable` | PostgreSQL connection string |
| `LISTEN_ADDR` | `:8080` | HTTP server bind address |
| `NO_AUTH` | (none, must be `"true"`) | Disable authentication for development |
| `LOG_LEVEL` | `info` | Log level: `debug`, `info`, `warn`, `error` |
| `LLM_PROVIDER` | `gemini` | LLM provider: `gemini` or `openai` |
| `LLM_MODEL` | `gemini-2.5-flash` (gemini) / `gpt-5.2` (openai) | Model name |
| `GOOGLE_API_KEY` | (empty) | Gemini API key (required for gemini provider) |
| `OPENAI_API_KEY` | (empty) | OpenAI API key (required for openai provider) |
| `OPENAI_BASE_URL` | `https://api.openai.com/v1` | OpenAI-compatible API endpoint |

---

### 9. NOTIFICATION CHANNELS (Routing)

**File:** `/internal/routing/`

Implementations of `Sender` interface:

| Channel Type | Implementation | Config Fields | Purpose |
|--------------|----------------|---------------|---------|
| `slack` | `SlackSender` (slack.go) | Webhook URL, channel name, etc. | Send notifications to Slack |
| `discord` | `DiscordSender` (discord.go) | Webhook URL, embed formatting | Send notifications to Discord |
| `webhook` | `WebhookSender` (webhook.go) | URL, HTTP method, headers | Send to arbitrary HTTP endpoints |

**Factory:** `routing.NewSenders()` creates all sender instances.
**Worker:** `NotifyWorker` uses senders to dispatch notifications when releases are detected.

---

### 10. INGESTION SOURCES

**Package:** `internal/ingestion/`

Implementations of `IIngestionSource` interface:

| Provider | Implementation | Mechanism | Key Method |
|----------|----------------|-----------|------------|
| `dockerhub` | `DockerHubSource` (dockerhub.go) | Polls Docker Hub API v2 | `FetchNewReleases()` queries `/v2/repositories/{repo}/tags/?page_size=25&ordering=last_updated` |
| `github` | `GitHubAtomSource` (github_atom.go) | Polls GitHub releases Atom feed | `FetchNewReleases()` parses `{repo}/releases.atom` XML |

**Orchestration:** `Orchestrator` in orchestrator.go polls enabled sources every ~5 minutes (configurable per source via `PollIntervalSeconds`).

**Service:** `Service.ProcessResults()` receives `IngestionResult` from sources and invokes `PgStore.IngestRelease()` (transactional outbox).

---

### 11. MAKEFILE TARGETS

**File:** `/Makefile`

| Target | Command | Purpose |
|--------|---------|---------|
| `up` | `docker compose up -d` + wait for Postgres | Start Postgres container |
| `down` | `docker compose down` | Stop containers |
| `db-reset` | Drop & recreate `changelogue` DB | Reset database |
| `build` | `go build -o changelogue ./cmd/server` | Compile server binary |
| `run` | `build` + start with `NO_AUTH=true` | Build and run server locally |
| `dev` | `up run` | One-command: start Postgres + run server |
| `test` | `go test ./...` | Run all unit tests |
| `vet` | `go vet ./...` | Lint with go vet |
| `lint` | `vet` | Alias for vet |
| `frontend-install` | `cd web && npm install` | Install frontend dependencies |
| `frontend-dev` | `cd web && npm run dev` | Start Vite dev server for Next.js |
| `frontend-build` | `cd web && npm run build` | Build static Next.js export |
| `integration-test` | `bash scripts/integration-test.sh` | Run integration tests (spins own Postgres on :5433) |
| `agent-dev` | `go run ./cmd/agent --project-id=...` | Run agent CLI for a specific project |
| `clean` | Remove binary + tear down containers/volumes | Full cleanup |

---

### 12. GO DEPENDENCIES (go.mod)

**Go version:** 1.25.0

**Key dependencies:**

| Package | Version | Purpose |
|---------|---------|---------|
| `github.com/riverqueue/river` | v0.31.0 | Job queue with PostgreSQL driver |
| `github.com/riverqueue/river/riverdriver/riverpgxv5` | v0.31.0 | River PostgreSQL v5 driver |
| `github.com/jackc/pgx/v5` | v5.8.0 | PostgreSQL driver (pure Go) |
| `google.golang.org/adk` | v0.5.0 | Agent Development Kit (orchestration) |
| `google.golang.org/genai` | v1.40.0 | Google Generative AI SDK (Gemini) |
| `github.com/google/uuid` | v1.6.0 | UUID generation |
| `golang.org/x/time` | v0.14.0 | Rate limiting utilities |

---

### 13. SERVER-SENT EVENTS (SSE) & BROADCASTER

**File:** `/internal/api/events.go`

**Architecture:**
- `Broadcaster` manages concurrent SSE client subscriptions
- PostgreSQL triggers (`notify_release_created`, `notify_semantic_release`) publish to `release_events` channel
- `ListenForNotifications()` (in main.go) bridges PostgreSQL LISTEN â†’ Broadcaster.Send()
- Clients connect via `GET /api/v1/events` which opens SSE stream
- Events fan-out to all connected clients (64-buffered channels, non-blocking send skips slow clients)

**Events:**
- `event: release` (data: JSON with `type: "release"`, `id`)
- `event: semantic_release` (data: JSON with `type: "semantic_release"`, `id`)
- `event: connected` (initial handshake)

---

### 14. AUTHENTICATION & AUTHORIZATION

**Mode 1: NO_AUTH (development)**
- Set `NO_AUTH=true` env var
- Disables all auth middleware
- Useful for local development

**Mode 2: API Key Authentication**
- Header: `Authorization: Bearer <api_key>`
- Validated via `KeyStore.ValidateKey()` (interface in `internal/api/auth.go`)
- Stored in `api_keys` table as hashed `key_hash` with `key_prefix` for display
- Rate-limited per key: 10 rps, 20 burst
- Usage tracked via `last_used_at` (async update)

---

### 15. FRONTEND PAGES

**Next.js App Router:** `/web/app/`

| Route | File | Purpose |
|-------|------|---------|
| `/` | `page.tsx` | Dashboard (home) |
| `/projects` | `page.tsx` | Projects list |
| `/projects/[id]` | `page.tsx` | Project detail |
| `/projects/[id]/agent` | `page.tsx` | Agent runs for project |
| `/projects/[id]/semantic-releases` | `page.tsx` | Semantic releases for project |
| `/projects/[id]/semantic-releases/[srId]` | `page.tsx` | Semantic release detail |
| `/releases` | `page.tsx` | All releases (global) |
| `/releases/[id]` | `page.tsx` | Release detail |
| `/semantic-releases` | `page.tsx` | All semantic releases (global) |
| `/sources` | `page.tsx` | Sources list |
| `/channels` | `page.tsx` | Notification channels |
| `/subscriptions` | `page.tsx` | Subscriptions |

**Components:** `/web/components/` organized by domain (agent, channels, dashboard, projects, releases, semantic-releases, sources, subscriptions, ui, layout, context-sources).

---

### 16. API HANDLER FILES

**File:** `/internal/api/` â€” 29 files organized by resource

Core route handlers:
- `projects.go`, `sources.go`, `releases.go`, `semantic_releases.go`
- `subscriptions.go`, `channels.go`, `context_sources.go`
- `agent.go`, `health.go`, `events.go`, `providers.go`

Supporting infrastructure:
- `server.go` â€” Route registration (`RegisterRoutes()`)
- `middleware.go` â€” HTTP middleware chain (RequestID, Logger, Recovery, RateLimit, Auth)
- `auth.go` â€” Key validation & rate limiting
- `response.go` â€” JSON response helpers (`RespondJSON`, `RespondError`, `RespondList`)
- `pgstore.go` â€” Unified data access implementation (all Store interfaces)
- `providers.go` â€” Static metadata (supported ingestion providers)

Test files (parallel _test.go):
- `*_test.go` â€” Unit tests for each handler

---

## SUMMARY

The ReleaseBeacon/Changelogue codebase is a well-architected release intelligence platform with:

1. **Clean layering:** API â†’ Service â†’ Store abstractions via interfaces
2. **Transactional integrity:** Outbox pattern for release ingestion
3. **Async orchestration:** River queue for notifications & agent runs
4. **Real-time events:** PostgreSQL LISTEN/NOTIFY â†’ SSE broadcaster
5. **Multi-channel routing:** Slack, Discord, webhooks
6. **Agent-driven analysis:** ADK-Go with Gemini/OpenAI LLMs
7. **Comprehensive API:** 50+ endpoints with auth, pagination, streaming
8. **Full CRUD:** Projects, sources, releases, semantic releases, channels, subscriptions, context sources

All documentation is in-line with code; no gaps found between implementation and current state.

1â†’package api
     2â†’
     3â†’import (
     4â†’	"net/http"
     5â†’
     6â†’	"github.com/jackc/pgx/v5/pgxpool"
     7â†’	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/ingestion"
     8â†’	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/routing"
     9â†’)
    10â†’
    11â†’// Dependencies holds all external dependencies required for registering API routes.
    12â†’type Dependencies struct {
    13â†’	DB                    *pgxpool.Pool
    14â†’	ProjectsStore         ProjectsStore
    15â†’	ReleasesStore         ReleasesStore
    16â†’	SubscriptionsStore    SubscriptionsStore
    17â†’	SourcesStore          SourcesStore
    18â†’	ChannelsStore         ChannelsStore
    19â†’	ContextSourcesStore   ContextSourcesStore
    20â†’	SemanticReleasesStore SemanticReleasesStore
    21â†’	AgentStore            AgentStore
    22â†’	KeyStore              KeyStore
    23â†’	HealthChecker         HealthChecker
    24â†’	Broadcaster           *Broadcaster
    25â†’	NoAuth                bool
    26â†’	IngestionService      *ingestion.Service
    27â†’	HTTPClient            *http.Client
    28â†’}
    29â†’
    30â†’// RegisterRoutes registers all API v1 routes on the given ServeMux.
    31â†’func RegisterRoutes(mux *http.ServeMux, deps Dependencies) {
    32â†’	// Public chain: no auth required.
    33â†’	publicChain := Chain(RequestID, Logger, Recovery)
    34â†’
    35â†’	// Authenticated chain: includes auth unless NoAuth is set.
    36â†’	chain := publicChain
    37â†’	if !deps.NoAuth {
    38â†’		chain = Chain(RequestID, Logger, Recovery, RateLimit(10, 20), Auth(deps.KeyStore))
    39â†’	}
    40â†’
    41â†’	// Projects (CRUD)
    42â†’	projects := NewProjectsHandler(deps.ProjectsStore)
    43â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.List)))
    44â†’	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Create)))
    45â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Get)))
    46â†’	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Update)))
    47â†’	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/projects/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(projects.Delete)))
    48â†’
    49â†’	// Sources (nested under projects)
    50â†’	sources := NewSourcesHandler(deps.SourcesStore, deps.IngestionService, deps.HTTPClient)
    51â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.List)))
    52â†’	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Create)))
    53â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Get)))
    54â†’	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Update)))
    55â†’	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.Delete)))
    56â†’	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/sources/{id}/poll", chain(http.HandlerFunc(sources.FetchReleases)))
    57â†’
    58â†’	// Context Sources (nested under projects)
    59â†’	contextSources := NewContextSourcesHandler(deps.ContextSourcesStore)
    60â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/context-sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.List)))
    61â†’	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/context-sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Create)))
    62â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Get)))
    63â†’	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Update)))
    64â†’	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/context-sources/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(contextSources.Delete)))
    65â†’
    66â†’	// Releases (read-only, nested under sources and projects)
    67â†’	releases := NewReleasesHandler(deps.ReleasesStore)
    68â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.List)))
    69â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/sources/{id}/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.ListBySource)))
    70â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.ListByProject)))
    71â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(releases.Get)))
    72â†’
    73â†’	// Semantic Releases (nested under projects + top-level list)
    74â†’	semanticReleases := NewSemanticReleasesHandler(deps.SemanticReleasesStore)
    75â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.ListAll)))
    76â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/semantic-releases", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.List)))
    77â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.Get)))
    78â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}/sources", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.ListSources)))
    79â†’	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/semantic-releases/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(semanticReleases.Delete)))
    80â†’
    81â†’	// Subscriptions (CRUD)
    82â†’	subscriptions := NewSubscriptionsHandler(deps.SubscriptionsStore)
    83â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/subscriptions", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.List)))
    84â†’	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/subscriptions", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Create)))
    85â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Get)))
    86â†’	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Update)))
    87â†’	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/subscriptions/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(subscriptions.Delete)))
    88â†’
    89â†’	// Channels (CRUD + test)
    90â†’	channels := NewChannelsHandler(deps.ChannelsStore, routing.NewSenders())
    91â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/channels", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.List)))
    92â†’	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/channels", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Create)))
    93â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Get)))
    94â†’	mux.Handle("PUT /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Update)))
    95â†’	mux.Handle("DELETE /api/v1/channels/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Delete)))
    96â†’	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/channels/{id}/test", chain(http.HandlerFunc(channels.Test)))
    97â†’
    98â†’	// Agent
    99â†’	agent := NewAgentHandler(deps.AgentStore)
   100â†’	mux.Handle("POST /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/agent/run", chain(http.HandlerFunc(agent.TriggerRun)))
   101â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/projects/{projectId}/agent/runs", chain(http.HandlerFunc(agent.ListRuns)))
   102â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/agent-runs/{id}", chain(http.HandlerFunc(agent.GetRun)))
   103â†’
   104â†’	// Providers (metadata â€” static, no store needed)
   105â†’	providers := NewProvidersHandler()
   106â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/providers", chain(http.HandlerFunc(providers.List)))
   107â†’
   108â†’	// SSE events
   109â†’	events := NewEventsHandler(deps.Broadcaster)
   110â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/events", chain(http.HandlerFunc(events.Stream)))
   111â†’
   112â†’	// Health (public â€” no auth middleware)
   113â†’	health := NewHealthHandler(deps.HealthChecker)
   114â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/health", publicChain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Check)))
   115â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/stats", chain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Stats)))
   116â†’	mux.Handle("GET /api/v1/stats/trend", chain(http.HandlerFunc(health.Trend)))
   117â†’}
   118â†’

1â†’package agent
     2â†’
     3â†’import (
     4â†’	"context"
     5â†’	"encoding/json"
     6â†’	"fmt"
     7â†’	"log/slog"
     8â†’	"time"
     9â†’
    10â†’	"github.com/riverqueue/river"
    11â†’	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/models"
    12â†’	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/queue"
    13â†’)
    14â†’
    15â†’// AgentWorker is a River worker that processes AgentJobArgs by running the
    16â†’// LLM agent orchestrator. When a job is dequeued, it loads the corresponding
    17â†’// agent run from the store and delegates to Orchestrator.RunAgent.
    18â†’type AgentWorker struct {
    19â†’	river.WorkerDefaults[queue.AgentJobArgs]
    20â†’	orchestrator *Orchestrator
    21â†’	store        OrchestratorStore
    22â†’}
    23â†’
    24â†’// NewAgentWorker creates a new AgentWorker backed by the given orchestrator
    25â†’// and store.
    26â†’func NewAgentWorker(orchestrator *Orchestrator, store OrchestratorStore) *AgentWorker {
    27â†’	return &AgentWorker{
    28â†’		orchestrator: orchestrator,
    29â†’		store:        store,
    30â†’	}
    31â†’}
    32â†’
    33â†’// Work processes a single AgentJobArgs job. It loads the agent run from the
    34â†’// database and runs the LLM agent through the orchestrator. If the project
    35â†’// has WaitForAllSources enabled and not all sources have the target version,
    36â†’// the job is snoozed for 5 minutes via river.JobSnooze.
    37â†’func (w *AgentWorker) Work(ctx context.Context, job *river.Job[queue.AgentJobArgs]) error {
    38â†’	slog.Info("agent worker picked up job",
    39â†’		"job_id", job.ID,
    40â†’		"agent_run_id", job.Args.AgentRunID,
    41â†’		"attempt", job.Attempt,
    42â†’	)
    43â†’
    44â†’	run, err := w.store.GetAgentRun(ctx, job.Args.AgentRunID)
    45â†’	if err != nil {
    46â†’		slog.Error("agent worker failed to load agent run",
    47â†’			"job_id", job.ID,
    48â†’			"agent_run_id", job.Args.AgentRunID,
    49â†’			"err", err,
    50â†’		)
    51â†’		return fmt.Errorf("get agent run: %w", err)
    52â†’	}
    53â†’
    54â†’	// Check multi-source waiting: if the run has a version and the project
    55â†’	// has WaitForAllSources enabled, verify all sources have reported a
    56â†’	// release for the target version before running the agent.
    57â†’	if run.Version != "" {
    58â†’		project, err := w.store.GetProject(ctx, run.ProjectID)
    59â†’		if err != nil {
    60â†’			slog.Error("agent worker failed to load project",
    61â†’				"job_id", job.ID,
    62â†’				"project_id", run.ProjectID,
    63â†’				"err", err,
    64â†’			)
    65â†’			return fmt.Errorf("get project: %w", err)
    66â†’		}
    67â†’
    68â†’		var rules models.AgentRules
    69â†’		if len(project.AgentRules) > 0 {
    70â†’			if err := json.Unmarshal(project.AgentRules, &rules); err != nil {
    71â†’				slog.Warn("agent worker: failed to unmarshal agent rules, proceeding without wait",
    72â†’					"project_id", run.ProjectID, "err", err)
    73â†’			}
    74â†’		}
    75â†’
    76â†’		if rules.WaitForAllSources {
    77â†’			ready, err := w.orchestrator.checkAllSourcesReady(ctx, run.ProjectID, run.Version)
    78â†’			if err != nil {
    79â†’				slog.Error("agent worker: error checking source readiness",
    80â†’					"job_id", job.ID,
    81â†’					"project_id", run.ProjectID,
    82â†’					"version", run.Version,
    83â†’					"err", err,
    84â†’				)
    85â†’				return fmt.Errorf("check all sources ready: %w", err)
    86â†’			}
    87â†’			if !ready {
    88â†’				// After maxWaitAttempts (12 Ă— 5min = ~1 hour), proceed
    89â†’				// with partial data rather than waiting forever.
    90â†’				const maxWaitAttempts = 12
    91â†’				if job.Attempt >= maxWaitAttempts {
    92â†’					slog.Warn("agent worker: max wait attempts reached, proceeding with partial data",
    93â†’						"job_id", job.ID,
    94â†’						"project_id", run.ProjectID,
    95â†’						"version", run.Version,
    96â†’						"attempts", job.Attempt,
    97â†’					)
    98â†’				} else {
    99â†’					slog.Info("agent worker: not all sources ready, snoozing",
   100â†’						"job_id", job.ID,
   101â†’						"project_id", run.ProjectID,
   102â†’						"version", run.Version,
   103â†’						"attempt", job.Attempt,
   104â†’					)
   105â†’					if err := w.store.UpdateAgentRunStatus(ctx, run.ID, "waiting"); err != nil {
   106â†’						slog.Error("failed to set waiting status", "err", err)
   107â†’					}
   108â†’					return river.JobSnooze(5 * time.Minute)
   109â†’				}
   110â†’			}
   111â†’		}
   112â†’	}
   113â†’
   114â†’	slog.Info("agent worker starting run",
   115â†’		"job_id", job.ID,
   116â†’		"agent_run_id", run.ID,
   117â†’		"project_id", run.ProjectID,
   118â†’		"trigger", run.Trigger,
   119â†’	)
   120â†’
   121â†’	if err := w.orchestrator.RunAgent(ctx, run); err != nil {
   122â†’		slog.Error("agent worker run failed",
   123â†’			"job_id", job.ID,
   124â†’			"agent_run_id", run.ID,
   125â†’			"project_id", run.ProjectID,
   126â†’			"err", err,
   127â†’		)
   128â†’		return err
   129â†’	}
   130â†’
   131â†’	slog.Info("agent worker run completed",
   132â†’		"job_id", job.ID,
   133â†’		"agent_run_id", run.ID,
   134â†’		"project_id", run.ProjectID,
   135â†’	)
   136â†’	return nil
   137â†’}
   138â†’

1â†’package main
     2â†’
     3â†’import (
     4â†’	"context"
     5â†’	"log/slog"
     6â†’	"net/http"
     7â†’	"os"
     8â†’	"os/signal"
     9â†’	"time"
    10â†’
    11â†’	"github.com/riverqueue/river"
    12â†’	agentpkg "github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/agent"
    13â†’	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/api"
    14â†’	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/db"
    15â†’	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/ingestion"
    16â†’	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/queue"
    17â†’	"github.com/sentioxyz/changelogue/internal/routing"
    18â†’)
    19â†’
    20â†’func main() {
    21â†’	// Configure log level from LOG_LEVEL env (debug, info, warn, error).
    22â†’	logLevel := new(slog.LevelVar) // defaults to Info
    23â†’	switch os.Getenv("LOG_LEVEL") {
    24â†’	case "debug":
    25â†’		logLevel.Set(slog.LevelDebug)
    26â†’	case "warn":
    27â†’		logLevel.Set(slog.LevelWarn)
    28â†’	case "error":
    29â†’		logLevel.Set(slog.LevelError)
    30â†’	}
    31â†’	slog.SetDefault(slog.New(slog.NewTextHandler(os.Stderr, &slog.HandlerOptions{Level: logLevel})))
    32â†’
    33â†’	ctx, stop := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt)
    34â†’	defer stop()
    35â†’
    36â†’	dbURL := envOr("DATABASE_URL", "postgres://localhost:5432/changelogue?sslmode=disable")
    37â†’	addr := envOr("LISTEN_ADDR", ":8080")
    38â†’	noAuth := os.Getenv("NO_AUTH") == "true"
    39â†’
    40â†’	// Database
    41â†’	pool, err := db.NewPool(ctx, dbURL)
    42â†’	if err != nil {
    43â†’		slog.Error("database connection failed", "err", err)
    44â†’		os.Exit(1)
    45â†’	}
    46â†’	defer pool.Close()
    47â†’
    48â†’	if err := db.RunMigrations(ctx, pool); err != nil {
    49â†’		slog.Error("migrations failed", "err", err)
    50â†’		os.Exit(1)
    51â†’	}
    52â†’
    53â†’	// API & routing store (created before River so we can register workers)
    54â†’	// The river client is set to nil initially; TriggerAgentRun (which needs it)
    55â†’	// won't be called until the server is up and the client is set below.
    56â†’	pgStore := api.NewPgStore(pool, nil)
    57â†’
    58â†’	// Register River workers
    59â†’	workers := river.NewWorkers()
    60â†’	notifyWorker := routing.NewNotifyWorker(pgStore)
    61â†’	river.AddWorker(workers, notifyWorker)
    62â†’
    63â†’	// Agent worker: requires an LLM API key. If unavailable, agent jobs
    64â†’	// will remain in the queue until the key is configured and the server
    65â†’	// is restarted.
    66â†’	llmProvider := envOr("LLM_PROVIDER", "gemini")
    67â†’	llmModelDefault := "gemini-2.5-flash"
    68â†’	if llmProvider == "openai" {
    69â†’		llmModelDefault = "gpt-5.2"
    70â†’	}
    71â†’	llmConfig := agentpkg.LLMConfig{
    72â†’		Provider:      llmProvider,
    73â†’		Model:         envOr("LLM_MODEL", llmModelDefault),
    74â†’		GoogleAPIKey:  os.Getenv("GOOGLE_API_KEY"),
    75â†’		OpenAIAPIKey:  os.Getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),
    76â†’		OpenAIBaseURL: os.Getenv("OPENAI_BASE_URL"),
    77â†’	}
    78â†’	agentOrchestrator, err := agentpkg.NewOrchestrator(pgStore, llmConfig)
    79â†’	if err != nil {
    80â†’		slog.Warn("agent orchestrator not available â€” agent jobs will not be processed", "err", err)
    81â†’	} else {
    82â†’		agentWorker := agentpkg.NewAgentWorker(agentOrchestrator, pgStore)
    83â†’		river.AddWorker(workers, agentWorker)
    84â†’		slog.Info("agent worker registered")
    85â†’	}
    86â†’
    87â†’	riverClient, err := queue.NewRiverClient(pool, workers)
    88â†’	if err != nil {
    89â†’		slog.Error("river client failed", "err", err)
    90â†’		os.Exit(1)
    91â†’	}
    92â†’
    93â†’	// Now that the river client exists, set it on the existing pgStore so that
    94â†’	// all holders (including NotifyWorker) can enqueue agent jobs.
    95â†’	pgStore.SetRiverClient(riverClient)
    96â†’
    97â†’	if err := riverClient.Start(ctx); err != nil {
    98â†’		slog.Error("river start failed", "err", err)
    99â†’		os.Exit(1)
   100â†’	}
   101â†’
   102â†’	// Ingestion layer
   103â†’	ingestionStore := ingestion.NewPgStore(pool, riverClient)
   104â†’	svc := ingestion.NewService(ingestionStore)
   105â†’
   106â†’	loader := ingestion.NewSourceLoader(pool, http.DefaultClient)
   107â†’	orch := ingestion.NewOrchestrator(svc, loader, pool, 5*time.Minute)
   108â†’
   109â†’	broadcaster := api.NewBroadcaster()
   110â†’
   111â†’	mux := http.NewServeMux()
   112â†’
   113â†’	// Register all API v1 routes
   114â†’	api.RegisterRoutes(mux, api.Dependencies{
   115â†’		DB:                    pool,
   116â†’		ProjectsStore:         pgStore,
   117â†’		ReleasesStore:         pgStore,
   118â†’		SubscriptionsStore:    pgStore,
   119â†’		SourcesStore:          pgStore,
   120â†’		ChannelsStore:         pgStore,
   121â†’		ContextSourcesStore:   pgStore,
   122â†’		SemanticReleasesStore: pgStore,
   123â†’		AgentStore:            pgStore,
   124â†’		KeyStore:              pgStore,
   125â†’		HealthChecker:         pgStore,
   126â†’		Broadcaster:           broadcaster,
   127â†’		NoAuth:                noAuth,
   128â†’		IngestionService:      svc,
   129â†’		HTTPClient:            http.DefaultClient,
   130â†’	})
   131â†’
   132â†’	srv := &http.Server{Addr: addr, Handler: api.CORS(mux)}
   133â†’
   134â†’	// Start polling in background
   135â†’	go orch.Run(ctx)
   136â†’
   137â†’	// Start PostgreSQL LISTEN/NOTIFY â†’ SSE bridge
   138â†’	go api.ListenForNotifications(ctx, pool, broadcaster)
   139â†’
   140â†’	// Start HTTP server
   141â†’	go func() {
   142â†’		slog.Info("server starting", "addr", srv.Addr)
   143â†’		if err := srv.ListenAndServe(); err != http.ErrServerClosed {
   144â†’			slog.Error("server error", "err", err)
   145â†’		}
   146â†’	}()
   147â†’
   148â†’	<-ctx.Done()
   149â†’	slog.Info("shutting down")
   150â†’
   151â†’	shutdownCtx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
   152â†’	defer cancel()
   153â†’	riverClient.Stop(shutdownCtx)
   154â†’	srv.Shutdown(shutdownCtx)
   155â†’}
   156â†’
   157â†’func envOr(key, fallback string) string {
   158â†’	if v := os.Getenv(key); v != "" {
   159â†’		return v
   160â†’	}
   161â†’	return fallback
   162â†’}
   163â†’

- **Does NOT** check agent rules â€” that happens in NotifyWorker

## 4. NotifyWorker in `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/internal/routing/worker.go`

**Validates your understanding:** NotifyWorker **does** check agent rules:
- Lines 93-94: Calls `w.checkAgentRules(ctx, release, job.Args.SourceID)` after sending notifications
- Lines 99-148: `checkAgentRules()` method:
  - Loads the source and project
  - Unparsals `project.AgentRules` JSON
  - Gets the previous release for comparison
  - Calls `CheckAgentRules(&rules, release.Version, previousVersion)`
  - If matched: enqueues an `AgentJobArgs` via `w.store.EnqueueAgentRun()`
  - Logs the trigger as `"auto:version:{version}"`

**Exact code for agent rule checking:**
```go
// Check agent rules and auto-trigger if criteria are met.
w.checkAgentRules(ctx, release, job.Args.SourceID)
```

And the full `checkAgentRules` method (lines 99-148) shows:
- Retrieves project's agent rules
- Gets previous release for version comparison
- Calls `CheckAgentRules()` to test version criteria
- Enqueues `EnqueueAgentRun()` if rule matches

## 5. LLM Provider Selection in `/Users/pc/web3/ReleaseBeacon/cmd/server/main.go`

**Lines 66-77 show provider selection:**
```go
llmProvider := envOr("LLM_PROVIDER", "gemini")
llmModelDefault := "gemini-2.5-flash"
if llmProvider == "openai" {
	llmModelDefault = "gpt-5.2"
}
llmConfig := agentpkg.LLMConfig{
	Provider:      llmProvider,
	Model:         envOr("LLM_MODEL", llmModelDefault),
	GoogleAPIKey:  os.Getenv("GOOGLE_API_KEY"),
	OpenAIAPIKey:  os.Getenv("OPENAI_API_KEY"),
	OpenAIBaseURL: os.Getenv("OPENAI_BASE_URL"),
}
```

**Logic:**
- Defaults to `"gemini"` with model `gemini-2.5-flash`
- If `LLM_PROVIDER=openai`, uses model `gpt-5.2` instead
- Passes all credentials to `NewOrchestrator()`
- If orchestrator initialization fails, agent jobs remain queued (line 80)

All your documentation assumptions are **correct and verified**.

![Build](https://github.com/sentioxyz/releaseguard/actions/workflows/ci.yml/badge.svg) ![AI Co-Authored](https://img.shields.io/endpoint?url=https://gist.githubusercontent.com/Poytr1/94cc8f0ddf90bb1d04dafbb76102786d/raw/ai-commits.json) ## What it does - **Discovers releases** by polling Docker Hub and GitHub on configurable intervals - **Routes notifications** to Slack, Discord, and webhooks the moment a new version lands - **Generates AI reports** via Google Gemini agents that research changelogs, assess risk, and summarize what changed - **Serves a dashboard** (Next.js) for managing projects, sources, subscriptions, and browsing releases in real time ## Architecture ```mermaid graph LR A["Ingestion
(polling)"] --> B["PostgreSQL +
River Queue"] B --> C["Agent
(ADK-Go)"] C --> D["Routing
(channels)"] A -.- A1["Docker Hub
GitHub"] B -.- B1["Transactional Outbox
LISTEN/NOTIFY â†’ SSE"] C -.- C1["Gemini LLM
research tools"] D -.- D1["Slack Â· Discord
Webhooks"] ``` Release insert and job enqueue happen in a single SQL transaction â€” zero-loss guarantee. See [ARCH.md](ARCH.md) and [DESIGN.md](DESIGN.md) for the full design. ## Quick start **Prerequisites:** Go 1.25+, Docker, Node.js 20+ ```bash # Start Postgres and the server (NO_AUTH mode) make dev # In another terminal â€” start the frontend make frontend-install make frontend-dev ``` The API runs on `localhost:8080`, the dashboard on `localhost:3000`. ## Environment variables | Variable | Default | Purpose | |----------|---------|---------| | `DATABASE_URL` | `postgres://localhost:5432/changelogue?sslmode=disable` | PostgreSQL connection | | `LISTEN_ADDR` | `:8080` | HTTP server bind address | | `GOOGLE_API_KEY` | _(empty)_ | Gemini API key for agent worker (disabled if unset) | ## Project structure ``` cmd/server/ Entry point â€” wires all layers together internal/ agent/ ADK-Go agent orchestrator, tools, and worker api/ REST API, SSE, middleware, auth db/ Connection pool and migrations ingestion/ Polling sources (Docker Hub, GitHub Atom) models/ Shared domain types queue/ River job definitions and client routing/ Notification channels and delivery worker web/ Next.js dashboard (React + Tailwind + shadcn) scripts/ Integration test harness ``` ## Extending More providers (npm, PyPI, Helm, etc.) and channels (PagerDuty, email, etc.) are planned. Adding one is a single-interface implementation: **Add a registry provider** â€” implement `IIngestionSource` in `internal/ingestion/source.go`: ```go type IIngestionSource interface { FetchNewReleases(ctx context.Context) ([]IngestionResult, error) } ``` **Add a notification channel** â€” implement `Sender` in `internal/routing/sender.go`: ```go type Sender interface { Send(ctx context.Context, channel models.NotificationChannel, msg Message) error } ``` ## Useful commands ```bash make build # go build -o changelogue ./cmd/server make test # go test ./... make vet # go vet ./... make integration-test # full integration test (spins up its own Postgres) make db-reset # drop and recreate the local database make clean # remove binary, stop containers, delete volumes ```

# Architecture: Changelogue

## Overview

Changelogue is an event-driven, hybrid-architecture system designed to centralize release discovery, automate validation, manage configurations, and distribute targeted notifications. It combines the high-concurrency performance of a Go-based polling engine with the reasoning capabilities of LLM-based SRE agents. By leveraging PostgreSQL for both persistent storage and message brokering, the system maintains high reliability and transactional consistency within a streamlined, single-binary deployment.

## 1. Tech Stack & Infrastructure

* **Backend / Engine:** Go (Golang). Chosen for its lightweight concurrency model (Goroutines) to handle simultaneous polling across multiple registries.
* **Frontend / Dashboard:** Next.js (React) with Tailwind CSS. Provides a fast, modern control center for viewing release streams and managing configurations.
* **Database, Queue, & Event Bus:** PostgreSQL.
* *Persistent Storage:* Standard relational tables for release metadata and system configurations.
* *Task Queue:* Utilizes `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` (via the **River** Go library) for robust, concurrent background job processing without race conditions.
* *Pub/Sub:* Utilizes native `LISTEN` and `NOTIFY` for real-time broadcasting to connected clients.


* **Intelligence Layer:** LLMs (Gemini / GPT-4o-mini) orchestrated via agent frameworks (e.g., LangGraph or Claude Agent SDK) for semantic changelog analysis and autonomous validation.
* **Packaging:** Single binary deployment utilizing Go's `//go:embed` to serve the Next.js static export directly from the Go server.

## 2. System Design & Abstractions

The system is decoupled into four primary layers communicating entirely through PostgreSQL using the Transactional Outbox pattern:

1. **Ingestion Layer** â€” Polling workers and webhook handlers discover new releases from upstream registries.
2. **Notification Routing** â€” River workers send immediate notifications to source-level subscribers on new releases.
3. **Agent Layer** â€” ADK-Go agents research releases, consult context sources, and produce semantic release reports.
4. **Routing & Output** â€” Notification channels (Slack, PagerDuty, webhooks) deliver alerts via `INotificationChannel`.

A **Project** is the central domain entity â€” it groups multiple ingestion sources, context sources, and notification subscriptions under a single tracked piece of software.

```mermaid
graph LR
    %% Data Flow
    subgraph Dashboard [API & Dashboard]
        API[REST API] --> |CRUD| PG
        API --> |SSE| Browser[Next.js Dashboard]
    end

    subgraph Ingestion [Ingestion Layer]
        I_Hub(Docker Hub Poller) --> |IIngestionSource| Engine
        I_Git(GitHub Webhooks) --> |HTTP Handler| Engine
    end

    subgraph Core [Event Bus & Queue - PostgreSQL]
        Engine((Go Engine)) --> |INSERT release + jobs\nTransactional Outbox| PG[(PostgreSQL)]
    end

    subgraph Notification [Notification Routing]
        PG --> |SKIP LOCKED\nRiver notify_release| NotifyWorker[Notify Worker]
        NotifyWorker --> |INotificationChannel| O_Slack(Slack/Teams)
        NotifyWorker --> |INotificationChannel| O_PD(PagerDuty)
        NotifyWorker --> |INotificationChannel| O_Hook(Custom Webhooks)
    end

    subgraph AgentLayer [Agent Layer]
        PG --> |SKIP LOCKED\nRiver agent_run| AgentWorker[Agent Worker]
        AgentWorker --> |ADK-Go| LLM[LLM Provider]
        AgentWorker --> |Research| ContextSources[Context Sources]
        AgentWorker --> |Produce| SemanticRelease[Semantic Release + Report]
    end

```

### Entity Relationship Model

```mermaid
erDiagram
    projects ||--o{ sources : "has many"
    projects ||--o{ context_sources : "has many"
    projects ||--o{ semantic_releases : "has many"
    projects ||--o{ agent_runs : "has many"
    sources ||--o{ releases : "produces"
    subscriptions }o--|| notification_channels : "routes to"
    subscriptions }o--o| sources : "source-level"
    subscriptions }o--o| projects : "project-level"
    semantic_releases ||--o{ semantic_release_sources : "composed of"
    releases ||--o{ semantic_release_sources : "contributes to"

    projects {
        uuid id PK
        string name UK
        text agent_prompt
        jsonb agent_rules
    }
    sources {
        uuid id PK
        uuid project_id FK
        string provider
        string repository UK
        boolean enabled
    }
    releases {
        uuid id PK
        uuid source_id FK
        string version
        jsonb raw_data
    }
    semantic_releases {
        uuid id PK
        uuid project_id FK
        string version
        jsonb report
        string status
    }
    agent_runs {
        uuid id PK
        uuid project_id FK
        uuid semantic_release_id FK
        string trigger
        string status
    }
    subscriptions {
        uuid id PK
        uuid channel_id FK
        string type
        uuid source_id FK
        uuid project_id FK
    }
    notification_channels {
        uuid id PK
        string type
        string name
        jsonb config
    }
```

### 2.1 The Event-Driven Backbone (PostgreSQL Only)

Components do not call each other synchronously. Instead, they rely on PostgreSQL to guarantee delivery:

* **The Transactional Outbox:** When a new release is detected, the ingestion worker writes the metadata to the `releases` table (linked to its `source_id`) and simultaneously enqueues River jobs (notification and/or agent) *within the exact same SQL transaction*. This guarantees no events are ever lost.
* **Real-time Pub/Sub:** Database triggers use `pg_notify` to broadcast lightweight events (e.g., telling the Next.js UI via SSE to refresh) over standard Postgres connections using `LISTEN`.
* **Reliable Queues:** River workers process jobs using `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`, ensuring exactly-once processing for both notification delivery and agent runs.
* **Project-Centric Model:** All data flows through the `projects` â†’ `sources` â†’ `releases` hierarchy. Subscriptions can attach at the source level (for raw release notifications) or the project level (for semantic release notifications).

### 2.2 Provider Interfaces (I/O)

All external integrations are abstracted behind strict Go interfaces.

* `IIngestionSource`: Standardizes how polling workers fetch data. Adding a new registry (like npm or NuGet) only requires implementing this interface. Each implementation maps to a `source_type` in the database.
* `INotificationChannel`: Standardizes output routing. Each implementation maps to a `type` in the `notification_channels` table (Slack, PagerDuty, custom webhooks).

### 2.3 REST API & Dashboard

The Go server exposes a RESTful API (`/api/v1`) serving the Next.js dashboard and external consumers:

* **Resource CRUD:** Projects, sources, subscriptions, notification channels â€” all manageable through the API.
* **Read-Only Releases:** Releases and semantic releases are queryable but not writable via the API â€” they're created exclusively through the ingestion layer and agent runs.
* **SSE Real-Time Events:** `GET /api/v1/events` streams server-sent events backed by PostgreSQL `LISTEN/NOTIFY`, pushing release and semantic release updates to connected dashboard clients.
* **API Key Auth:** Bearer token authentication with hashed key storage. Webhooks use their own HMAC-based auth.
* **Rate Limiting:** Per-key token bucket with standard `X-Ratelimit-*` response headers.

### 2.4 Notification Routing

When a new source release is detected, a `notify_release` River job is enqueued in the same transaction as the release insert. The notification worker:

1. Resolves all source-level subscriptions for the release's source.
2. For each subscription, sends a notification to the linked channel via `INotificationChannel`.

This provides immediate, low-latency alerts for raw releases without waiting for AI analysis. Source-level subscriptions are simple: "tell me when this source has a new release."

### 2.5 Agent Layer (Implemented)

For project-level intelligence, an `agent_run` River job triggers an LLM agent (via ADK-Go v0.5.0) that:

1. Gathers recent source releases for the project using the `get_releases` tool.
2. Inspects individual release details (changelogs, raw data) using `get_release_detail`.
3. Consults context sources (runbooks, docs, monitoring dashboards) using `list_context_sources`.
4. Produces a `SemanticRelease` with a structured `SemanticReport` (summary, availability, adoption, urgency, recommendation).

Agent behavior is configured per-project via `agent_prompt` (custom instructions) and `agent_rules` (structured triggers like `on_major_release`, `on_security_patch`, `version_pattern`). Agent runs are tracked in the `agent_runs` table for observability and auditability.

**Implementation details (`internal/agent/`):**

* **`tools.go`** â€” Three ADK function tools (`get_releases`, `get_release_detail`, `list_context_sources`) created via `functiontool.New()`. Tools are scoped to a project via `toolFactory` which holds the `AgentDataStore` and project ID.
* **`orchestrator.go`** â€” `Orchestrator` manages the full agent lifecycle: loading project config, creating an ADK `llmagent` with Gemini as the LLM backend, running the agent via `runner.Runner`, parsing the JSON report, and persisting the `SemanticRelease` in a transaction.
* **`worker.go`** â€” `AgentWorker` implements `river.Worker[queue.AgentJobArgs]`, loading the agent run from the store and delegating to `Orchestrator.RunAgent()`.
* **Graceful degradation** â€” If `GOOGLE_API_KEY` is not set, the orchestrator is not created, the worker is not registered, and a warning is logged. The rest of the system continues to function. Agent jobs remain in the River queue until the key is configured.

### 2.6 Agentic Tooling (Planned: SRE Validation)

For deep validation, Changelogue will utilize SRE agents with a suite of abstracted tools:

* `UpgradeBaseABoxConfig(version)`
* `CheckAgentStatus(environment)`
* `SyncOpsOpsack(payload)`
This will allow the agent to autonomously deploy a sandbox, verify that the deployment is healthy, and rollback or alert on failure.

## 3. Data Flow: Lifecycle of a Release Event

1. **Discovery:** A Go worker polling Docker Hub detects a new base image tag for a configured source (e.g., the "Docker Hub" source under the "Go Runtime" project). Source-level exclusion filters (`exclude_version_regexp`, `exclude_prereleases`) are applied -- filtered versions are discarded immediately.
2. **Ingestion & Transaction:** The worker stores the raw upstream payload and executes a single database transaction to insert the record into `releases` (linked to its `source_id`) and enqueue River jobs (`notify_release` for immediate notifications, optionally `agent_run` if the project's agent rules match).
3. **Notification (Source-Level):** A River worker picks up the `notify_release` job, resolves source-level subscriptions, and sends alerts to configured notification channels (Slack, PagerDuty, webhooks).
4. **Agent Analysis (Project-Level):** If triggered, a River worker picks up the `agent_run` job. The ADK-Go agent gathers recent releases, consults context sources, and produces a `SemanticRelease` with a structured report (summary, availability, adoption, urgency, recommendation).
5. **Broadcast:** PostgreSQL triggers fire `NOTIFY` payloads on release insert and semantic release completion, pushing SSE events to connected dashboard clients.
6. **Project-Level Notification:** When a semantic release is completed, project-level subscribers are notified with the AI-generated report (e.g., a critical PagerDuty alert for high-urgency releases, or a batched daily digest to Slack for low-priority updates).

## 4. Directory Structure

```text
/changelogue
â”śâ”€â”€ cmd/
â”‚   â””â”€â”€ server/          # Main Go application entry point
â”śâ”€â”€ internal/
â”‚   â”śâ”€â”€ api/             # REST API handlers, middleware, SSE broadcaster
â”‚   â”śâ”€â”€ ingestion/       # Polling workers and webhook handlers (IIngestionSource)
â”‚   â”śâ”€â”€ agent/           # ADK-Go agent for semantic release analysis
â”‚   â”śâ”€â”€ notification/    # Notification routing worker and channel implementations
â”‚   â”śâ”€â”€ queue/           # PostgreSQL River queue setup and job definitions
â”‚   â”śâ”€â”€ db/              # Connection pool and schema migrations
â”‚   â””â”€â”€ models/          # Shared domain structs (Release, Project, SemanticRelease, etc.)
â”śâ”€â”€ web/                 # Next.js frontend application
â”‚   â”śâ”€â”€ app/
â”‚   â””â”€â”€ components/
â”śâ”€â”€ deployments/         # Dockerfiles and Base A Box integration scripts
â””â”€â”€ go.mod

```

-* **Intelligence Layer:** LLMs (Gemini / GPT-4o-mini) orchestrated via agent frameworks (e.g., LangGraph or Claude Agent SDK) for semantic changelog analysis and autonomous validation.
+* **Intelligence Layer:** LLMs (Gemini / OpenAI-compatible) orchestrated via [ADK-Go](https://google.github.io/adk-go/) v0.5.0 for semantic changelog analysis and autonomous release evaluation.
* **Packaging:** Single binary deployment utilizing Go's `//go:embed` to serve the Next.js static export directly from the Go server.

# Architecture: Changelogue

## Overview

Changelogue is an event-driven, hybrid-architecture system designed to centralize release discovery, automate validation, manage configurations, and distribute targeted notifications. It combines the high-concurrency performance of a Go-based polling engine with the reasoning capabilities of LLM-based SRE agents. By leveraging PostgreSQL for both persistent storage and message brokering, the system maintains high reliability and transactional consistency within a streamlined, single-binary deployment.

## 1. Tech Stack & Infrastructure

* **Backend / Engine:** Go (Golang). Chosen for its lightweight concurrency model (Goroutines) to handle simultaneous polling across multiple registries.
* **Frontend / Dashboard:** Next.js (React) with Tailwind CSS. Provides a fast, modern control center for viewing release streams and managing configurations.
* **Database, Queue, & Event Bus:** PostgreSQL.
* *Persistent Storage:* Standard relational tables for release metadata and system configurations.
* *Task Queue:* Utilizes `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED` (via the **River** Go library) for robust, concurrent background job processing without race conditions.
* *Pub/Sub:* Utilizes native `LISTEN` and `NOTIFY` for real-time broadcasting to connected clients.


* **Intelligence Layer:** LLMs (Gemini / OpenAI-compatible) orchestrated via [ADK-Go](https://google.github.io/adk-go/) v0.5.0 for semantic changelog analysis and autonomous release evaluation.
* **Packaging:** Single binary deployment utilizing Go's `//go:embed` to serve the Next.js static export directly from the Go server.

## 2. System Design & Abstractions

The system is decoupled into four primary layers communicating entirely through PostgreSQL using the Transactional Outbox pattern:

1. **Ingestion Layer** â€” Polling workers and webhook handlers discover new releases from upstream registries.
2. **Notification Routing** â€” River workers send immediate notifications to source-level subscribers on new releases.
3. **Agent Layer** â€” ADK-Go agents research releases, consult context sources, and produce semantic release reports.
4. **Routing & Output** â€” Notification channels (Slack, Discord, webhooks) deliver alerts via `Sender`.

A **Project** is the central domain entity â€” it groups multiple ingestion sources, context sources, and notification subscriptions under a single tracked piece of software.

```mermaid
graph LR
    %% Data Flow
    subgraph Dashboard [API & Dashboard]
        API[REST API] --> |CRUD| PG
        API --> |SSE| Browser[Next.js Dashboard]
    end

    subgraph Ingestion [Ingestion Layer]
        I_Hub(Docker Hub Poller) --> |IIngestionSource| Engine
        I_Git(GitHub Webhooks) --> |HTTP Handler| Engine
    end

    subgraph Core [Event Bus & Queue - PostgreSQL]
        Engine((Go Engine)) --> |INSERT release + jobs\nTransactional Outbox| PG[(PostgreSQL)]
    end

    subgraph Notification [Notification Routing]
        PG --> |SKIP LOCKED\nRiver notify_release| NotifyWorker[Notify Worker]
        NotifyWorker --> |INotificationChannel| O_Slack(Slack/Teams)
        NotifyWorker --> |INotificationChannel| O_PD(PagerDuty)
        NotifyWorker --> |INotificationChannel| O_Hook(Custom Webhooks)
    end

    subgraph AgentLayer [Agent Layer]
        PG --> |SKIP LOCKED\nRiver agent_run| AgentWorker[Agent Worker]
        AgentWorker --> |ADK-Go| LLM[LLM Provider]
        AgentWorker --> |Research| ContextSources[Context Sources]
        AgentWorker --> |Produce| SemanticRelease[Semantic Release + Report]
    end

```

### Entity Relationship Model

```mermaid
erDiagram
    projects ||--o{ sources : "has many"
    projects ||--o{ context_sources : "has many"
    projects ||--o{ semantic_releases : "has many"
    projects ||--o{ agent_runs : "has many"
    sources ||--o{ releases : "produces"
    subscriptions }o--|| notification_channels : "routes to"
    subscriptions }o--o| sources : "source-level"
    subscriptions }o--o| projects : "project-level"
    semantic_releases ||--o{ semantic_release_sources : "composed of"
    releases ||--o{ semantic_release_sources : "contributes to"

    projects {
        uuid id PK
        string name UK
        text agent_prompt
        jsonb agent_rules
    }
    sources {
        uuid id PK
        uuid project_id FK
        string provider
        string repository UK
        boolean enabled
    }
    releases {
        uuid id PK
        uuid source_id FK
        string version
        jsonb raw_data
    }
    semantic_releases {
        uuid id PK
        uuid project_id FK
        string version
        jsonb report
        string status
    }
    agent_runs {
        uuid id PK
        uuid project_id FK
        uuid semantic_release_id FK
        string trigger
        string status
    }
    subscriptions {
        uuid id PK
        uuid channel_id FK
        string type
        uuid source_id FK
        uuid project_id FK
    }
    notification_channels {
        uuid id PK
        string type
        string name
        jsonb config
    }
```

### 2.1 The Event-Driven Backbone (PostgreSQL Only)

Components do not call each other synchronously. Instead, they rely on PostgreSQL to guarantee delivery:

* **The Transactional Outbox:** When a new release is detected, the ingestion worker writes the metadata to the `releases` table (linked to its `source_id`) and simultaneously enqueues River jobs (notification and/or agent) *within the exact same SQL transaction*. This guarantees no events are ever lost.
* **Real-time Pub/Sub:** Database triggers use `pg_notify` to broadcast lightweight events (e.g., telling the Next.js UI via SSE to refresh) over standard Postgres connections using `LISTEN`.
* **Reliable Queues:** River workers process jobs using `FOR UPDATE SKIP LOCKED`, ensuring exactly-once processing for both notification delivery and agent runs.
* **Project-Centric Model:** All data flows through the `projects` â†’ `sources` â†’ `releases` hierarchy. Subscriptions can attach at the source level (for raw release notifications) or the project level (for semantic release notifications).

### 2.2 Provider Interfaces (I/O)

All external integrations are abstracted behind strict Go interfaces.

* `IIngestionSource`: Standardizes how polling workers fetch data. Adding a new registry (like npm or NuGet) only requires implementing this interface. Each implementation maps to a `source_type` in the database.
* `INotificationChannel`: Standardizes output routing. Each implementation maps to a `type` in the `notification_channels` table (Slack, PagerDuty, custom webhooks).

### 2.3 REST API & Dashboard

The Go server exposes a RESTful API (`/api/v1`) serving the Next.js dashboard and external consumers:

* **Resource CRUD:** Projects, sources, subscriptions, notification channels â€” all manageable through the API.
* **Read-Only Releases:** Releases and semantic releases are queryable but not writable via the API â€” they're created exclusively through the ingestion layer and agent runs.
* **SSE Real-Time Events:** `GET /api/v1/events` streams server-sent events backed by PostgreSQL `LISTEN/NOTIFY`, pushing release and semantic release updates to connected dashboard clients.
* **API Key Auth:** Bearer token authentication with hashed key storage. Webhooks use their own HMAC-based auth.
* **Rate Limiting:** Per-key token bucket with standard `X-Ratelimit-*` response headers.

### 2.4 Notification Routing

When a new source release is detected, a `notify_release` River job is enqueued in the same transaction as the release insert. The notification worker:

1. Resolves all source-level subscriptions for the release's source.
2. For each subscription, sends a notification to the linked channel via `INotificationChannel`.

This provides immediate, low-latency alerts for raw releases without waiting for AI analysis. Source-level subscriptions are simple: "tell me when this source has a new release."

### 2.5 Agent Layer (Implemented)

For project-level intelligence, an `agent_run` River job triggers an LLM agent (via ADK-Go v0.5.0) that:

1. Gathers recent source releases for the project using the `get_releases` tool.
2. Inspects individual release details (changelogs, raw data) using `get_release_detail`.
3. Consults context sources (runbooks, docs, monitoring dashboards) using `list_context_sources`.
4. Produces a `SemanticRelease` with a structured `SemanticReport` (summary, availability, adoption, urgency, recommendation).

Agent behavior is configured per-project via `agent_prompt` (custom instructions) and `agent_rules` (structured triggers like `on_major_release`, `on_security_patch`, `version_pattern`). Agent runs are tracked in the `agent_runs` table for observability and auditability.

**Implementation details (`internal/agent/`):**

* **`tools.go`** â€” Three ADK function tools (`get_releases`, `get_release_detail`, `list_context_sources`) created via `functiontool.New()`. Tools are scoped to a project via `toolFactory` which holds the `AgentDataStore` and project ID.
* **`orchestrator.go`** â€” `Orchestrator` manages the full agent lifecycle: loading project config, creating an ADK `llmagent` with Gemini as the LLM backend, running the agent via `runner.Runner`, parsing the JSON report, and persisting the `SemanticRelease` in a transaction.
* **`worker.go`** â€” `AgentWorker` implements `river.Worker[queue.AgentJobArgs]`, loading the agent run from the store and delegating to `Orchestrator.RunAgent()`.
* **Graceful degradation** â€” If `GOOGLE_API_KEY` is not set, the orchestrator is not created, the worker is not registered, and a warning is logged. The rest of the system continues to function. Agent jobs remain in the River queue until the key is configured.

### 2.6 Agentic Tooling (Planned: SRE Validation)

For deep validation, Changelogue will utilize SRE agents with a suite of abstracted tools:

* `UpgradeBaseABoxConfig(version)`
* `CheckAgentStatus(environment)`
* `SyncOpsOpsack(payload)`
This will allow the agent to autonomously deploy a sandbox, verify that the deployment is healthy, and rollback or alert on failure.

## 3. Data Flow: Lifecycle of a Release Event

1. **Discovery:** A Go worker polling Docker Hub detects a new base image tag for a configured source (e.g., the "Docker Hub" source under the "Go Runtime" project). Source-level exclusion filters (`exclude_version_regexp`, `exclude_prereleases`) are applied -- filtered versions are discarded immediately.
2. **Ingestion & Transaction:** The worker stores the raw upstream payload and executes a single database transaction to insert the record into `releases` (linked to its `source_id`) and enqueue River jobs (`notify_release` for immediate notifications, optionally `agent_run` if the project's agent rules match).
3. **Notification (Source-Level):** A River worker picks up the `notify_release` job, resolves source-level subscriptions, and sends alerts to configured notification channels (Slack, PagerDuty, webhooks).
4. **Agent Analysis (Project-Level):** If triggered, a River worker picks up the `agent_run` job. The ADK-Go agent gathers recent releases, consults context sources, and produces a `SemanticRelease` with a structured report (summary, availability, adoption, urgency, recommendation).
5. **Broadcast:** PostgreSQL triggers fire `NOTIFY` payloads on release insert and semantic release completion, pushing SSE events to connected dashboard clients.
6. **Project-Level Notification:** When a semantic release is completed, project-level subscribers are notified with the AI-generated report (e.g., a critical PagerDuty alert for high-urgency releases, or a batched daily digest to Slack for low-priority updates).

## 4. Directory Structure

```text
/changelogue
â”śâ”€â”€ cmd/
â”‚   â””â”€â”€ server/          # Main Go application entry point
â”śâ”€â”€ internal/
â”‚   â”śâ”€â”€ api/             # REST API handlers, middleware, SSE broadcaster
â”‚   â”śâ”€â”€ ingestion/       # Polling workers and webhook handlers (IIngestionSource)
â”‚   â”śâ”€â”€ agent/           # ADK-Go agent for semantic release analysis
â”‚   â”śâ”€â”€ notification/    # Notification routing worker and channel implementations
â”‚   â”śâ”€â”€ queue/           # PostgreSQL River queue setup and job definitions
â”‚   â”śâ”€â”€ db/              # Connection pool and schema migrations
â”‚   â””â”€â”€ models/          # Shared domain structs (Release, Project, SemanticRelease, etc.)
â”śâ”€â”€ web/                 # Next.js frontend application
â”‚   â”śâ”€â”€ app/
â”‚   â””â”€â”€ components/
â”śâ”€â”€ deployments/         # Dockerfiles and Base A Box integration scripts
â””â”€â”€ go.mod

```

I_Hub(Docker Hub Poller) --> |IIngestionSource| Engine
-        I_Git(GitHub Webhooks) --> |HTTP Handler| Engine
+        I_Git(GitHub Atom Feed) --> |IIngestionSource| Engine
end
subgraph Core [Event Bus & Queue - PostgreSQL]

        NotifyWorker --> |INotificationChannel| O_PD(PagerDuty)
        NotifyWorker --> |INotificationChannel| O_Hook(Custom Webhooks)
